name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
desc:
Lay on decline bench with both arms extended in front of you.
Break at elbows and bring your upper arms to side.
Once your upper arms have reached your side, press barbell back to ceiling.

name: Barbell Bench Press
desc:
Lay flat on the bench with your feet on the ground. With straight arms unrack the bar.
Lower the bar to your mid chest
Raise the bar until you've locked your elbows.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
desc:
Position the bench between 30 and 45 degrees.
Lay flat on the bench with your feet on the ground. With straight arms unrack the bar.
Lower the bar to your mid chest
Raise the bar (slowly and controlled) until you've locked your elbows.

name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Press
desc:
Lay on decline bench with both arms extended in front of you.
Break at elbows and bring your upper arms to side.
Once your upper arms have reached your side, press dumbbells back to ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
desc:
Start by lying flat on bench with dumbbell in hand.
Hold dumbbells at chest level with palms facing forward.
Engage your core and press dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.

name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
desc:
Lay flat on incline bench with your feet on ground. Raise dumbbells until you have straight arms.
Lower dumbbells to your mid chest
Raise dumbbells until you"ve locked your elbows.

name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
desc:
Lay flat on bench and place your feet on ground.
Begin exercise with dumbbells held together above your chest, elbows slightly bent.
Simultaneously lower weights to either side.
Pause when weights are parallel to bench, then raise your arms to starting position.

name: Dumbbell Incline Chest Fly
desc:
Lay on incline bench with two dumbbells extended in front of you.
You should have slight bend in your elbows and maintain bend. Shoulder joint should do all movement.
Bring dumbbells out to your side. Stop dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring dumbbells back together at top.

name: Dumbbell Squeeze Press
desc:
Lay flat on bench with two dumbbells pressed to one another.
Break at elbows and keep your elbows tucked as you bring dumbbells to your chest.
Continue to press dumbbells into one another throughout rep.
Once dumbbells reach chest, press back toward ceiling.

name: Low-to-High Dumbbell Fly
desc:
Stand holding two dumbbells in each hand.
You should have slight bend in your elbows and maintain bend. Shoulder joint should do all movement.
Bring dumbbells out to your side. Stop dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring dumbbells back together right in front of your chest squeezing chest intensely.

name: Dumbbell Weighted Dip
desc:
Use dip belt to attach dumbbell to yourself. Grip bars with arms straight, shoulders above hands. Keep legs straight or bent.
Lower slowly until elbows are at 90 degrees.
Push back up to start position, extending arms fully.

name: Machine Plate Loaded Incline Chest Press
desc:
Adjust the seat pad to match your height.
Press the weight forward until your elbows are nearly extended. Return to the starting position with out letting the weight rest at the bottom until finishing your set.

name: Machine Pec Fly
desc:
Adjust seat height so handles align with chest. Sit and grip handles with elbows slightly bent.
Slowly bring handles together in front, keeping tension. Focus on squeezing chest muscles.
Return handles to starting position with controlled motion. Keep chest up throughout. Repeat for reps.

name: Cable Decline Bench Press
desc:
Use a handle attachment. Bring a bench over to the cables. The cables should be set above height.
Bring both of the handles to your chest and make sure you are in the center of the cable crossover.
From there, you should flex and extend at both the shoulders and elbows simultaneously.

name: Cable Bench Press
desc:
Use a handle attachment. Bring a bench over to the cables. The cables should be set to shoulder height.
Bring both of the handles to your chest and make sure you are in the center of the cable crossover.
From there, you should flex and extend at both the shoulders and elbows simultaneously.

name: Cable Incline Bench Press
desc:
Use a handle attachment. Bring a bench over to the cables. The cables should be set fairly low.
Bring both of the handles to your chest and make sure you are in the center of the cable crossover.
From there, you should flex and extend at both the shoulders and elbows simultaneously.

name: Push Up
desc:
Place your hands firmly on the ground, directly under shoulders.
Flatten your back so your entire body is straight and slowly lower your body
Draw shoulder blades back and down, keeping elbows tucked close to your body
Exhale as you push back to the starting position.

name: Incline Push Up
desc:
Stand facing bench or sturdy elevated platform.
Place hands on edge of bench or platform, slightly wider than shoulder width.
Slowly lower your body until your chest almost touches the bench.
Push body up until arms are extended.

name: Decline Push Up
desc:
Use a bench to elevate your feet.
Put your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Slowly lower your body until your chest almost touches the ground
Raise your body until you almost lock your elbows.

name: Knee Push Ups
desc:
Begin in a push-up position on your knees. Break at the elbow and shoulder joint.
Lower your body, keeping elbows close.
Push back up to the starting position.

name: Deadlift
desc:
Stand with your mid-foot under the bar and grip the bar with your hands, about a shoulder width apart.
Bend your knees, then lift the bar by straightening your back. It is important to keep your back straight.
Stand to your full height and hold.
Lower the bar to the floor by bending your knees and keeping your back straight.

name: Kneeling Single Arm Row
desc:
Place one hand and one knee on a bench. Make sure you keep a flat back.
Pull the dumbbell toward the ceiling.
Make sure to avoid any jerking or rotating.

name: Pronated Row
desc:
Grab the barbell with an overhand grip. Hinge forward while keeping your back flat until your torso is about parallel with the ground.
Let your arms hang freely.
Pull the bar to your torso while tucking your elbows in slightly.
Extend your elbows fully before initiating the next rep.

name: Pulldown
desc:
Grip the bar with the palms facing forward, your hands need to be spaced out at a distance wider than shoulder width.
As you have both arms extended in front of you holding the bar, bring your torso back around 30 degrees while sticking your chest out.
Pull the bar down to about chin level or a little lower in a smooth movement whilst squeezing the shoulder blades together.
After a second of squeezing, slowly raise the bar back to the starting position when your arms are fully extended.

name: Underhand Pulldown
desc:
Use the Straight Bar attachment. Hold with an underhand grip.
Hold the attachment as you sit on the machine and put your legs under the leg pads.
Pull the bar to your clavicle, then raise back up until your arm are extended.

name: Neutral Pulldown
desc:
Use the V Bar attachment.
Hold the attachment as you sit on the machine and put your legs under the leg pads.
Pull the bar to your clavicle, then raise back up until your arm are extended.

name: Seated Cable Row
desc:
Sit with your back straight on the machine and grip the handles.
Pull the handles back using your arms. Your legs and torso should be at a 90° angle. Push out your chest.
Pull the handles towards your body until your hands are beside your abdomen.

name: Landmine T Bar Rows
desc:
Grab a v bar and place it under the bar's collar.
Hinge forward with a flat back and pull your elbows straight back toward the ceiling.
Fully extend your elbows before initiating the next rep.

name: Pullover
desc:
Grab a dumbbell and hold with both hands. Point your dumbbell at the ceiling.
Flex at the shoulder joint while keeping your elbows mostly extended.
Flex until you feel a stretch in your lats then bring the dumbbell back to the ceiling.

name: Laying Incline Row
desc:
Lay on an incline bench that is between 30-45 degrees high.
Let your arms hang freely. Pull your elbows straight back toward the ceiling.

name: Machine Plate Loaded Row
desc:
Sit on the machine, feet flat, and grab the handles.
Pull handles towards your lower abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep elbows close to your body.
Slowly return to the starting position, fully extending your arms without locking elbows. Repeat for desired reps.

name: Cable Rop Pullover
desc:
Use a rope attachment. Cable should be set all the way to the top of the machine.
Grab your attachment and walk a few steps back. Push your butt back to where you are leaning forward somewhat.
Initiate the movement with your shoulders and not your elbows. Imagine trying to pull the attachment through your thighs at the bottom.

name: Cable Bent Over Bar Pullover
desc:
You can use any attachment. Cable should be set all the way to the top of the machine.
Grab your attachment and walk a few steps back. Push your butt back to where you are leaning forward somewhat.
Initiate the movement with your shoulders and not your elbows. Imagine trying to pull the attachment through your thighs at the bottom.

name: Silverback Shrug
desc:
Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding dumbbells with both hands in front just past shoulder width.
Bend forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, keeping your back straight.
Engage your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to touch them together.

name: Silverback Barbell Shrug
desc:
Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding the Barbell with both hands in front just past shoulder width.
Bend forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, keeping your back straight.
Engage your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to touch them together.
Release the shrug.

name: 30 Degree Shrug
desc:
Hold barbell with a wide grip. Your arms should be at a roughly 30 degree angle.
Pull your shoulders towards your ears instead of straight up toward the ceiling.

name: Back Extension
desc:
Position your thighs on the padding and hook your feet on the platform supports.
Keeping your back straight, slowly bend at your waist until your legs and back are at a 45° angle.
Slowly raise your body to the starting position.

name: Pull Ups
desc:
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, arms and shoulders fully extended.
Pull your body up until your chin is above the bar.
Lower your body back to starting position.

name: Assisted Pull Up
desc:
Adjust weight for assistance. Stand on platform, grip handles above shoulder-width. Knees on pad if available.
Slowly pull up until chin is above the bar. Keep elbows close, core engaged.
Lower back with control. Pause at bottom. Repeat for reps.

name: Weighted Pull Ups
desc:
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, arms and shoulders fully extended. Weight hanging between your legs.
Pull your body up until your chin is above the bar.
Lower your body back to starting position.

name: Overhead Press
desc:
Take a roughly shoulder width grip. There should be a straight line from your elbow to fist (vertical forearms).
Pull your chin back and press the weight toward the ceiling by extending at the elbow joint and flexing at the shoulder joint.
Press until your elbows are extended and push your head forward slightly.
Return to the start position with control. Pulling your chin back to allow the bar to pass your face safely.

name: Seated Overhead Press
desc:
Sit on a bench with back support. Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height with your palms forward.
Raise the dumbbells upwards and pause at the contracted position.
Lower the weights back to starting position.

name: Upright Row
desc:
Take a double overhand roughly shoulder width grip.
Pull your elbows straight up the ceiling. Aim to get the bar to chin level or slightly higher.

name: Front Raise
desc:
Grab two dumbbells while standing upright with the dumbbells at your side.
Raise the two dumbbells with your elbows being fully extended until the dumbbells are eye level.
Lower the weights in a controlled manner to the starting position and repeat.

name: Plate Front Raise
desc:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the torso, palms facing the thighs.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, lift the plate up to the front of the body.
Raise the plate up until the arms are fully extended, maintaining control of the plate.
Lower the plate back to the starting position and repeat for desired reps.

name: Cable Bar Front Raise
desc:
Face away from the cables.
With your elbows extended raise your fists straight up to the ceiling until they are at eye level.

name: Cable Rope Front Raise
desc:
Face away from the cables. Use a rope attachment.
With your elbows extended raise your fists straight up to the ceiling until they are at eye level.

name: Arnold Press
desc:
Stand with both palms facing you.
Begin to press the dumbbells to the ceiling while rotating your palms.
When you reach elbow extension, your palms should be facing forward.

name: Lateral Raise
desc:
Stand up straight with dumbbells at either side, palms facing your hips.
Raise your arms on either side with a slight bend in your elbow until they are parallel with the floor. Pause at the top of the motion.
Slowly return your arms down to starting position.

name: Bayesian Lateral Raise
desc:
Lay on an incline bench and let your arms freely.
Lateral raise your arms straight out to the side until your arms are about parallel with the ground.

name: Standing Bayesian Lateral Raise
desc:
Stand tall on lean slightly forward.
Lateral raise your arms straight out to the side until your arms are about parallel with the ground.

name: Cable Low Single Arm Lateral Raise
desc:
Use a handle attachment with the cable set all the way to the bottom of the machine.
You should vertically abduct at the shoulder raising your arm straight out to the side.
Raise until your arm is parallel with the ground and then back to the starting position.

name: Cable Rope Face Pulls
desc:
Facing the pulley, pull the weight towards you while keeping your arms parallel to the ground.
Pull your hands back to both sides of your head and hold the position.
Slowly return weight to starting position. Repeat.

name: Machine Reverse Fly
desc:
Sit on machine and press your chest into the pad.
Grab the handles with an overhand grip. Keep your elbows mostly extended.
Bring your arms backwards and squeeze your scapula together.

name: Seated Rear Delt Fly
desc:
With dumbbells in either hand, bend your knees with your feet slightly bowed out. Arch your back above your knees, and start with the weights touching in front of your chest.
With bent elbows, raise your arms up to shoulder level, pausing at the at the end of the motion.
Slowly lower your arms back to starting position.

name: Cable High Internally Rotated Reverse Fly
desc:
Use a handle attachment or no attachment at all. The cable should be set to the top.
Walk a few steps away with the cable coming from the opposing side of the working arms. Internally rotate the shoulders.
From there, your shoulders should horizontally abduct and adduct, while your elbows stay in a fixed position.

name: Rope Kneeling Face Pull
desc:
Start with the cables at the top. Use a rope attachment. Fall to a half kneeling position.
Pull the metal piece in the middle of the rope to the bridge of your nose.

name: Seated Rear Delt Row
desc:
Sit on a bench. Put your chest in your lap. Try to get your torso as close to parallel with the ground as your mobility will allow for.
Let your arms hang in front of you. Pull your elbows back towards the ceiling while flaring your elbows outward.

name: Cable External Rotation
desc:
Hold the cable handle in one hand, elbow bent at 90 degrees going across your torso.
Rotate your arm outward, keeping your elbow stable.

name: Barbell Curl
desc:
While holding the upper arms stationary, curl the weights forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out.
Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a second and squeeze the biceps hard.
Slowly bring the weight back down to the starting position.

name: Preacher Curl
desc:
Sit at preacher bench, hold dumbbells in each hand with arm extended, palm facing up.
Curl dumbbells towards shoulder, keeping upper arm stationary and elbows in.
Lower dumbbell slowly back to start.

name: Hammer Curl
desc:
Hold the dumbbells with a neutral grip (thumbs facing the ceiling).
Slowly lift the dumbbell up to chest height
Return to starting position and repeat.

name: Cable Rope Hammer Curl
desc:
Use a rope attachment. The cable should be set all the way to the bottom of the machine.
Face away from the cable machine. Stagger your stance so you have a better base of support.
Use a neutral grip thumb facing up pinky-facing down). Flex at the elbow and extend.

name: Cable Bayesian Curl
desc:
Use a handle attachment. The cable should be set all the way to the bottom of the machine.
Face away from the cable machine.
Stagger your stance so you have a better base of support. Face your palm forward. Flex at the elbow and extend.

name: EZ Bar Preacher Curl
desc:
Position yourself on preacher bench; grasp EZ bar with underhand grip, elbows extended.
Slowly curl bar towards shoulders; keep upper arms stationary, focus on biceps.
Lower bar back to starting position; maintain control, repeat for reps.

name: Incline Curl
desc:
Hold the dumbbells with an underhand grip. Seated on an incline bench.
Slowly lift the dumbbell up to chest height
Return to starting position and repeat.

name: Cable Bar Curl
desc:
Use a bar attachment. The cable should be set all the way to the bottom of the machine.
Face the cable machine.
Stagger your stance so you have a better base of support. Face your palm forward. Flex at the elbow and extend.

name: Cable Rope Overhead Tricep Extension
desc:
You can use any attachment. The cable should be all the way to the bottom of the machine. Face away from the machine.
Point your elbows straight up toward the ceiling.
From there, extend your elbows until your fists are pointed straight toward the ceiling.

name: Barbell Skullcrusher
desc:
Take a shoulder width grip. Break at the elbows. Try to keep your elbows tucked in. Don't let them flare out.
Stop the bar a few inches from your forehead and extend your elbows back up.

name: Dumbbell Seated Overhead Tricep Extension
desc:
Sit on the bench and hold a dumbbell with both hands. Raise the dumbbell overhead at arms length, holding the weight up with the palms of your hands.
Keep your elbows in while you lower the weight behind your head, your upper arms stationary.
Raise the weight back to starting position.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
desc:
Stand tall with a single dumbbell extended overhead.
Flex at the elbow letting the dumbbell travel behind your body.
Try not to let your elbow flare outward.

name: Cable Rope Pushdown
desc:
The cable should be set all the way at the top of the machine.
Make sure to keep your upper arm glued at your side. Extend your elbows until you feel your triceps contract.

name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
desc:
Lay flat on the bench with your feet on the ground. With a narrow grip on the bar, straighten your arms
Lower the bar to your lower-mid chest
Slowly raise the bar until you've locked your elbows.

name: Bench Dips
desc:
Grip the edge of the bench with your hands, Keep your feet together and legs straight.
Lower your body straight down.
Slowly press back up to the starting point.
TIP: Make this harder by raising your feet off the floor and adding weight.

name: Diamond Push Ups
desc:
Position your index fingers and thumbs so they are touching, forming a diamond shape
Use a standard push up position
Lower your chest towards your hands, keep your elbows close to your body
Stop just before your chest touches the floor, then push back up to the starting position.

name: Cable Bar Pushdown
desc:
Use the bar attachment. The cable should be set all the way at the top of the machine.
Make sure to keep your upper arm glued at your side. Extend your elbows until you feel your triceps contract.

name: Cable Tricep Kickback
desc:
Use a handle attachment. The cable should be set to hip height.
Hinge forward. Make sure to keep your upper arm glued at your side. Extend your elbows until you feel your triceps contract.

name: Cable Single Arm Rope Pushdown
desc:
Use the rope attachment. The cable should be set all the way at the top of the machine.
Make sure to keep your upper arm glued at your side. Extend your elbows until you feel your triceps contract.

name: Cable Single Arm Pushdown
desc:
Use a handle attachment. The cable should be set all the way at the top of the machine.
Make sure to keep your upper arm glued at your side. Extend your elbow until you feel your tricep contract.

name: Barbell Squat
desc:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Maintain the natural arch in your back, squeezing your shoulder blades and raising your chest.
Grip the bar across your shoulders and support it on your upper back. Unwrack the bar by straightening your legs, and take a step back.
Bend your knees as you lower the weight without altering the form of your back until your hips are below your knees.
Raise the bar back to starting position, lift with your legs and exhale at the top.

name: Smith Machine Squat
desc:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Maintain the natural arch in your back and put the bar on your back. Squeezing your shoulder blades and raise your chest.
Grip the bar across your shoulders and support it on your upper back. Unwrack the smith machine by straightening your legs.
Bend your knees as you lower the weight without altering the form of your back until your hips are below your knees.
Raise the bar back to starting position, lift with your legs and exhale at the top.

name: Smith Machine Sissy Squat
desc:
Stand facing away from the machine, positioning your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart to provide a stable base, and then reach behind you to firmly grip the bar with both hands, maintaining an overhand grip.
Initiate the movement by leaning backward, allowing your upper body to incline while simultaneously bending your knees. As you descend, maintain control
Drive through your quadriceps while pushing your hips forward, gradually straightening your knees. Maintain a slow, controlled pace

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
desc:
Find a box or bench that is roughly knee height or a few inches lower. (The lower the box is the lower the mobility requirement).
You can either place your toes or the top of your foot on the box. Point your front foot forward or slightly turned outwards and break at the knees and hips simultaneously.
Aim to get your front leg to hit parallel depth (hamstring parallel with the ground) or lower.
The further forward your front foot is, the longer the Range of Motion is at your hips. The further back your front foot is, the longer the ROM at your knees.

name: Machine Hip Adduction
desc:
Sit in the machine with your legs apart, pads against your outer thighs. Adjust the seat and pad position comfortably.
Slowly squeeze your legs together, focusing on using your Quads muscles. Pause when the pads are close together.
Slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid letting the weights slam. Repeat for desired reps and sets.

name: Forward Lunges
desc:
Step forward with one leg.
Lower your body until your rear knee nearly touches the ground.
Ensure you remain upright, and your front knee stay above the front foot.
Push off the floor with your front foot until you return to the starting position. Switch legs

name: Machine Leg Extension
desc:
Sit on the machine with your back against the cushion and adjust the machine you are using so that your knees are at a 90 degree angle at the starting position.
Raise the weight by extending your knees outward, then lower your leg to the starting position. Both movements should be done in a slow, controlled motion.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
desc:
Hold the weight tucked into your upper chest area, keeping your elbows in. Your feet should be slightly wider than shoulder width.
Sink down into the squat, keeping your elbows inside the track of your knees.
Push through your heels while keeping your chest up and return to starting position.

name: Machine Leg Press
desc:
Place your legs on the platform with your feet at shoulder width.
Release the weight and extend your legs fully, without locking your knees.
Lower the weight until your legs are at a 90° angle (but DO NOT allow your butt and lower back to rise off of the pad. This will put your lower back in a rounded position, which is very dangerous.)
Raise the weight back to starting position.

name: Barbell Stiff Leg Deadlifts
desc:
Stand with a barbell at your shins with your feet shoulder width apart.
Bend forward at your hips and keep your knees as fully extended as possible.nGrab the barbell and then extend your hips while maintaining a straight back.
From the standing position, lower the weight in a controlled manner.
You can either lower the weight to the floor or before you touch the floor, depending on your mobility.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
desc:
Stand with a shoulder width stance. Push your butt back while leaving your knees MOSTLY extended.
You should feel a stretch in your hamstrings. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward to complete the rep.
Do not push your hips all the way forward. This will hyperextend your spine. Just go to a normal standing position.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
desc:
Take a shoulder width, double overhand or mixed grip.
Push your hips back while leaving your knees mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

name: Machine Hamstring Curl
desc:
Lay down on the machine, placing your legs beneath the padded lever. Position your legs so that the padded lever is below your calve muscles.
Support yourself by grabbing the side handles of the machine, and slowly raise the weight with your legs, toes pointed straight.
Pause at the apex of the motion, then slowly return to starting position.

name: Machine Seated Hamstring Curl
desc:
Adjust the back pad and ankle pad to suit your height.
Disengage the knee pad and lower it as far as you can.
Flex your knees against resistance, then extend until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

name: Machine Seated Calf Raises
desc:
Get comfortable on the machine, then place your lower thighs beneath the padded lever. Place your toes and the balls of your feet onto the foot supports.
Prevent the weight from slipping forward by gripping the handles, and release the safety bar. Lower the weight until your calves are extended.
Push your heels up to lift the padded lever and hold the contracted position, then slowly lower back down to the starting position. Repeat.

name: Machine Standing Calf Raises
desc:
Adjust the machine in accordance with your height and place your shoulders underneath the padded lever.
The balls of your feet should be supporting your weight on the calve block, your heels extending off of it.
Extend your heels upwards while keeping your knees stationary, and pause at the contracted position.
Slowly return to the starting position. Repeat.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
desc:
Sit on the ground with a bench behind you. Have the barbell over your legs just above your hips.
Lean back against the bench so that your shoulders are resting upon it, stretch your arms out to either side using the bench as support.
Raise the weight by driving through your feet and extending your hips upwards. Support the weight with your shoulders and feet.
Slowly extend as far as you can, and then slowly return to the starting position.

name: Stationary Bike
desc:
Hold the handles and maintain a neutral spine.
Pedal at a moderate pace and try to minimise rocking back and forth.

name: Jump Rope
desc:
Hold handles, elbows close, and jump with both feet together.
Rotate wrists to swing the rope over your head.
Jump as it reaches your feet, keeping a steady rhythm.

name: Jumping Jacks
desc:
Stand with feet together, arms at your sides, ready to jump.
Jump while spreading legs and raising arms overhead.
Return to starting position with legs together and arms at your sides. Repeat.

name: Criss Cross Jacks
desc: Perform a jumping jack while crossing the feet.

name: Knee Taps
desc:
Stand straight, feet hip-width apart, engage core.
Lift knees alternately, tap with same side hand.
Continue rhythmically, maintain posture, increase speed as able.

name: Treadmill Walk
desc: Get the treadmill up to a speed where you are doing a walk.

name: Treadmill Sprint
desc: Get the treadmill up to a speed where you are doing a sprint.

name: Stair Climber
desc:
Step onto step, grip handles lightly, maintain tall posture and neutral spine.
Drive through midfoot, alternate steps smoothly, avoid leaning heavily on handles.
Set moderate pace, breathe rhythmically, keep knees tracking over toes.
